en.extract:
The olive-backed tailorbird (Orthotomus sepium) is a species of passerine bird formerly placed in the "Old World warbler" assemblage, it but now placed in the family Cisticolidae.
It is endemic to the islands of Java, Madura Island, Bali and Lombok. It resides in open forests, often perching on short trees and shrubs.


Description
It is small, with a rust-colored head.


Diet
It is insectivorous. It forages for caterpillars, spiders, and small insects. 


Social Behavior
It is often seen in groups, and is known to engage in group vocalizations. 


References

The '''olive-backed tailorbird''' ('''''Orthotomus sepium''''') is a species of [[Passerine|passerine bird]] formerly placed in the "[[Old World warbler]]" assemblage, it but now placed in the family [[Cisticolidae]].

It is endemic to the islands of [[Java]], [[Madura Island]], [[Bali]] and [[Lombok]]. It resides in open forests, often perching on short trees and shrubs.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last1=Zulhariadi |first1=Muhammad |last2=Irawan |first2=Raden Dedi |last3=Zulfaeda |first3=Aulia |last4=Hidayani |first4=Nurul |date=2022-08-26 |title=Dragonflies Diversity and Land Cover Changes in the Batubolong River, West Lombok District |journal=Biotropia |volume=29 |issue=2 |pages=112–123 |doi=10.11598/btb.2022.29.2.1637 |issn=1907-770X|doi-access=free }}</ref>

== Description ==
It is small, with a rust-colored head.<ref name=":0" />

== Diet ==
It is insectivorous.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Marianingsih |first1=Pipit |last2=Mahrawi |first2=Mahrawi |last3=Cahyani |first3=Adira |last4=Octoviani |first4=Riesta |last5=Nurrohmah |first5=Syifa |last6=Nugraha |first6=Lailli Sabta |last7=Karahayon |first7=Yoseph Sugiharto |last8=Febrio |first8=Eren Putra |date=2023-06-14 |title=Biology Learning Content Analysis on Aves Diversity: A Case Study at Kaduberureum Biodiversity Park's PT Chandra Asri Petrochemical Tbk, Indonesia |journal=International Journal of Biology Education Towards Sustainable Development |volume=3 |issue=1 |pages=32–51 |doi=10.53889/ijbetsd.v3i1.201 |issn=2809-5073|doi-access=free }}</ref> It forages for<ref>{{Cite book |last1=Rohman |first1=Fatchur |last2=Diwanata |first2=Bima |last3=Akhsani |first3=Farid |last4=Priambodo |first4=Bagus |last5=Lestari |first5=Sri Rahayu |chapter=Exploring biodiversity of Indonesian birds with their plant preferences |date=2020 |volume=2231 |page=040086 |title= INTERNATIONAL CONFERENCE ON LIFE SCIENCES AND TECHNOLOGY (ICoLiST)|chapter-url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1063/5.0002506 |publisher=AIP Publishing |doi=10.1063/5.0002506}}</ref> caterpillars, spiders, and small insects.<ref name=":0" /> 

== Social Behavior ==
It is often seen in groups, and is known to engage in group vocalizations.<ref name=":0" />
